target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: Fix smart-quoted tool call argument repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #67488.
- Fixes smart-quoted malformed tool-call argument JSON so the embedded runner can recover write/edit args that use Unicode curly quote delimiters.
- Adds delimiter-aware normalization in `src/shared/balanced-json.ts` while preserving valid JSON and existing balanced-prefix/trailing-junk repair behavior.
- Covers the stream-wrapper path plus shared scanner edge cases for CJK/Markdown content, inner smart quotes, raw newlines, invalid/literal backslashes, and trailing backslashes.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed the change and understand the affected tool-call argument repair path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed smart-quoted tool-call arguments that previously left write/edit tool arguments empty are repaired before tool execution observes them.

Real environment tested: local Node.js v22.22.0 loading the TypeScript source with `tsx`, exercising the actual embedded-runner `wrapStreamFnRepairMalformedToolCallArguments` stream wrapper with a synthetic streamed write tool call.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script importing `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-argument-repair.ts`, streaming `.functions.write:0 ` plus smart-quoted JSON-like args, draining the wrapped stream, and printing repaired partial/streamed/end/final tool-call args. The local proof log is `.artifacts/67488-smart-quote-repair-proof.log`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
node_version: v22.22.0
json_parse_contract: SyntaxError Expected property name or '}' in JSON at position 1 (line 1 column 2)
repair_gate_openai_completions: true
[agent/embedded] repairing malformed tool call arguments with 18 leading chars and 0 trailing chars
result_is_final_message: true
partial_args: {"path":"notes/报告.md","backupDir":"C:\\tmp\\","content":"更新 **草稿** with “smart”, “sure” and code \"x\"\nconst re = /\\d+/;\n内部内容"}
streamed_args: {"path":"notes/报告.md","backupDir":"C:\\tmp\\","content":"更新 **草稿** with “smart”, “sure” and code \"x\"\nconst re = /\\d+/;\n内部内容"}
end_message_args: {"path":"notes/报告.md","backupDir":"C:\\tmp\\","content":"更新 **草稿** with “smart”, “sure” and code \"x\"\nconst re = /\\d+/;\n内部内容"}
final_args: {"path":"notes/报告.md","backupDir":"C:\\tmp\\","content":"更新 **草稿** with “smart”, “sure” and code \"x\"\nconst re = /\\d+/;\n内部内容"}
content_preserved: true
backup_dir_preserved: true
args_were_empty_before_repair: true
```

Observed result after fix: the wrapper repairs the malformed argument object and writes identical args into the partial, streamed, end-message, and final tool-call blocks; Node still rejects the raw structural-smart-quote JSON directly, proving the repair is needed.

What was not tested: live cron delivery, live Telegram, and real provider credentials were not used. The representative failing runtime path is the embedded-runner malformed tool-call argument repair boundary, and the proof uses synthetic content only.

## Root Cause

The existing repair path tried `JSON.parse(raw)` and then `extractBalancedJsonPrefix(raw)`. Both paths only understood ASCII `"` JSON string delimiters. Provider output with Unicode smart quotes as structural delimiters is visually JSON-like but invalid under `JSON.parse`, and braces/brackets inside smart-quoted edit content could also confuse the balanced-prefix scanner.

## Dependency contract

Node/ECMAScript `JSON.parse` accepts JSON strings delimited with ASCII double quotes, not Unicode smart quotes. Local proof on Node v22.22.0 rejects `{“path”:“notes/report.md”}` with `SyntaxError: Expected property name or '}' in JSON at position 1`.

The internal helper contract for `extractBalancedJsonPrefix` is to find the first balanced `{` or `[` JSON-like fragment while ignoring braces and brackets inside recognized strings. This patch extends that scanner to recognize likely smart-quoted malformed strings for balancing, and adds a separate normalizer for parse repair.

## Regression Test Plan

- Shared scanner/normalizer coverage in `src/shared/balanced-json.test.ts`.
- Embedded-runner stream repair coverage in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-argument-repair.test.ts`.
- Existing broad runner coverage in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`.

## Security Impact

No credential, auth, sandbox, or network behavior changes. The repair remains bounded to the existing malformed tool-call argument gate and synthetic parser recovery. Raw valid JSON is parsed first and preserved.

## Human Verification

Pending final author review before PR creation. The local mini-guide for the affected architecture and proof is `.artifacts/issue-67488-smart-quote-repair-guide.html`.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Checked `.github/CODEOWNERS`; the touched files do not match a special owner-restricted pattern. Expect normal maintainer review for `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/*` and `src/shared/*`.

## Changelog

No contributor `CHANGELOG.md` edit. Maintainers can add the release note during landing if needed.

## Review conversations

No GitHub PR review conversations yet. Local `codex review --base origin/main` initially found edge cases for raw quotes/newlines/backslashes and then returned no actionable correctness issues after fixes. Related closed PR #67551 and bot feedback were reviewed to avoid the rejected obsolete-scanner fix shape.

## Risks

- Smart-quote repair is heuristic because malformed JSON has no complete formal grammar.
- `src/shared/balanced-json.ts` is shared with CLI-output JSON fragment extraction, so scanner behavior was kept context-driven and covered by focused tests.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- Smart quotes are treated as structural delimiters only in JSON-like positions and when the following token looks like a JSON boundary.
- Literal straight quotes, raw control characters, and literal backslashes inside smart-quoted strings are escaped so parsed content is preserved.
- The existing provider/model API repair gate is unchanged.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change cron state accounting, delivery status, or error persistence.
- Does not expand repair to direct `openai-responses` transports.
- Does not change already-valid ASCII-delimited JSON tool arguments.
- Does not add dependencies or change package metadata.

## Validation

```text
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm test src/shared/balanced-json.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-argument-repair.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
node --import tsx --input-type=module <synthetic stream repair proof>
```

target.title: refactor: centralize inbound supplemental context
target.body:
## Summary

Moves more inbound context responsibility into core. Channel plugins now pass canonical supplemental/facts payloads; core owns visibility filtering, media payload projection, lazy quote-media merge, self-reply suppression, and legacy prompt-field projection.

What changed:

- `finalizeInboundContext` accepts `MsgContext.SupplementalContext` and projects it into existing `ReplyTo*`, `Forwarded*`, `Thread*`, `GroupSystemPrompt`, and untrusted-context fields before prompt normalization.
- `finalizeChannelInboundContext` centralizes supplemental filtering, media payload merge, optional async quote-media resolution, self-quote body/media suppression, injected finalizer support, and hidden quote/forward/thread diagnostics.
- `buildChannelInboundEventContext` routes through the core finalizer while preserving the public sync/async SDK contract: sync by default, `Promise` only for `resolveSupplementalMedia: true`.
- Bundled/internal channel callers moved to `buildChannelInboundEventContext` or `runtime.channel.turn.buildContext`; direct finalizer usage is now a deprecated public compatibility island.
- Discord and Telegram await `buildChannelInboundEventContext({ resolveSupplementalMedia: true })`; channel code still owns native fetch/parsing, core owns merge/suppression/finalization.
- Matrix, Feishu, iMessage, WhatsApp dispatch, QQBot, Slack, MS Teams, Signal, Google Chat, Synology Chat, Tlon, Twitch, Zalo, and Zalo User now use the builder/turn-builder path for inbound context payload construction.
- `resolveChannelInboundSupplementalContext` remains exported as a deprecated SDK wrapper for compatibility.
- Deprecated SDK type aliases remain exported so external plugin TypeScript imports keep compiling.
- Preserved existing legacy flat fields; supplemental projection fills missing values only.
- Added regression coverage for WhatsApp remote-only inbound media URLs.

API/deprecation notes:

- Deprecated: `filterChannelTurnSupplementalContext` alias.
- Deprecated: `resolveChannelInboundSupplementalContext`; prefer `buildChannelInboundEventContext({ resolveSupplementalMedia: true })` for inbound payloads.
- Deprecated: `finalizeChannelInboundContext` for new channel code; kept for public compatibility when callers already prepared legacy prompt fields.
- Preferred: `buildChannelInboundEventContext` / `runtime.channel.turn.buildContext` for channel inbound payloads.
- Not deprecated: legacy `ReplyTo*`, `Forwarded*`, and `Thread*` fields. They remain runtime/template compatibility fields and are now projected by core.

Diff/LOC notes:

- Current PR diff vs merge-base `origin/main`: +1575 / -784, net +791.
- Production/runtime-ish triple-dot diff, excluding tests/docs/changelog: +1088 / -553, net +535.
- Final local dirty slice added by the last migration commit: +404 / -240 overall; prod-ish +306 / -166, net +140.
- This is not a LOC win. The win is the ownership boundary: bundled channels stop owning prompt projection/finalization, while public SDK compatibility remains intact.

## Verification

Latest proof after rebase, SDK compatibility fix, and final builder migration:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-compat-1.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-compat-2.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.systemPrompt.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.body-for-agent.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts extensions/zalouser/src/monitor.group-gating.test.ts`
  - passed: 12 files, 200 tests after final rebase on `origin/main` `9b9d8970b0`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - passed: 1 file, 46 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts`
  - passed: 3 files, 22 tests
- `git diff --check`
  - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search --output /tmp/inbound-refactor-autoreview-async-builder-3.txt --json-output /tmp/inbound-refactor-autoreview-async-builder-3.json`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch is correct

Earlier focused channel proof during this refactor:

- Core finalization + reply inbound tests passed: 2 files, 28 tests.
- Matrix/MS Teams focused tests passed: 2 files, 25 tests.
- WhatsApp inbound context/dispatch tests passed before the final regression addition: 2 files, 48 tests.
- Signal/iMessage focused tests passed: 3 files, 48 tests.
- Telegram focused shared-builder and media tests passed.
- Discord focused context/process-message tests passed: 2 files, 77 tests.
- Feishu targeted quote/thread tests passed; QQBot full outbound-dispatch tests passed.

## Real behavior proof

Observed result after fix: Channels pass canonical `SupplementalContext`/facts or builder facts into core; `finalizeInboundContext` projects legacy fields once; lazy reply media is resolved by the finalizer path only when visible and not self-suppressed; remote-only WhatsApp inbound media URLs remain present in finalized context.

What was not tested: Full repository test suite, full CI completion, and live channel roundtrips were not run for this refactor PR.

target.title: fix(gateway): log hook rejection diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add centralized, sanitized warning logs for expected Gateway hook 4xx rejections.
- Preserve existing hook HTTP statuses and response bodies while adding stable rejection reason codes.
- Add focused regression coverage for body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation rejection logs.

Fixes #69192.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected Gateway hook request path, including `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts`, `src/gateway/server/hooks-rejection-log.ts`, `src/gateway/hooks.ts`, and the focused tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway hook requests that return 4xx now emit one sanitized warning line with stable diagnostic fields, so operators can diagnose body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation failures from the journal/log.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway runtime on loopback with throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; fake hook token; no real credentials.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug \
  pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 33583 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured --auth none --verbose

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/wake \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-invalid-json' \
    --data-binary 'not-json'

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/agent \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-direct-invalid-channel' \
    --data-binary '{"message":"hello from proof","channel":"last","agent":"proof"}'

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/mapped-message \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-mapped-scalar' \
    --data-binary '"hello from scalar proof"'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Copied live output and redacted runtime log excerpt from the isolated Gateway run:

  ```text
  invalid_json status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"Unexpected token 'o', \"not-json\" is not valid JSON"}
  direct_validation status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"channel must be last"}
  mapped_scalar status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"hook mapping requires message"}

  2026-05-25T14:56:38.517-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=wake code=400 reason=body-invalid-json method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=unparsed body-bytes=8 request-id=proof-invalid-json
  2026-05-25T14:56:38.529-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=agent code=400 reason=agent-channel-invalid method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=object body-bytes=58 request-id=proof-direct-invalid-channel
  2026-05-25T14:56:38.542-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=mapped-message code=400 reason=mapping-requires-message method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=string body-bytes=23 request-id=proof-mapped-scalar

  log_leak_check=PASS
  status_checks=PASS
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The three representative hook 4xx responses remained 400, and each emitted a journal-visible `[hooks] hook rejected ...` warning with path, status code, stable reason, method, content type, body type, bounded body length, and request id. The warning lines did not contain the fake token or sensitive payload strings.

- What was not tested: Live third-party webhook senders, successful hook dispatch into an agent run, and external channel delivery. The changed surface is rejection diagnostics; the proof intentionally avoided real credentials and live integrations.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof used loopback Gateway runtime and throwaway state/config only. It did not use real hook credentials or live external integrations.

## Root Cause

The hook request handler returned expected 4xx responses from several branches without a corresponding rejection log. Body-read failures, direct `wake`/`agent` validation failures, mapped hook validation failures, policy/session-key failures, and final hook 404s therefore had user-visible failures but weak operator diagnostics.

## Dependency contract

`src/gateway/hooks.ts` wraps `src/infra/http-body.ts`. The body reader returns stable limit codes and current response strings:

- `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` -> `payload too large`
- `REQUEST_BODY_TIMEOUT` -> `request body timeout`
- `CONNECTION_CLOSED` -> `Connection closed`
- invalid JSON -> parser error text

The patch maps these existing strings to stable hook rejection reasons without changing the response body or status mapping.

`src/logging/subsystem.ts` accepts `logHooks.warn(message, meta)`. Because console/journal logs surface the message most directly, the patch puts the sanitized key/value diagnostics in the warning message and also passes structured metadata.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` now covers warning logs for request body timeout, invalid JSON, direct validation rejection, and mapped validation rejection.
- The tests assert stable reason codes, request ids, body types, bounded body length, and absence of fake tokens/raw payload strings in warning messages and metadata.
- `src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts` now asserts stable mapping reasons for missing agent message and wake text.

## Security Impact

Positive. The new logs improve diagnosability without logging Authorization headers, hook tokens, raw request bodies, rendered hook messages, channel targets, or session keys. Header-derived log values are normalized to one line and length bounded. Body length is reported from `content-length` only and capped.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the affected hook ingress path, the body reader contract, and the logging helper contract. I also reviewed and accepted the `codex review --base origin/main` finding about scalar body-type inference, then reran tests, changed checks, and review. After rebasing onto current upstream main, I reran the focused tests, changed gate, and isolated Gateway proof.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

`.github/CODEOWNERS` has secops ownership for gateway auth/secret/security-specific files, but no direct required owner for these touched hook handler/mapping/test files. Gateway maintainer review is still recommended because this changes hook ingress logging semantics.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor PR; maintainer/AI landing should add the release changelog entry if accepted.

## Review conversations

- Checked issue #69192 and exact open/closed PR searches.
- No open PR linked to or substantially addressing #69192 was found.
- Reviewed closed PR #69267 as prior art; it was closed unmerged and had broader/unrelated changes.
- Ran `codex review --base origin/main`; accepted the scalar body-type finding.
- Reran `codex review --base origin/main`; it reported no discrete actionable regressions.

## Risks

- The patch intentionally adds one warning log for each expected hook 4xx rejection, which may increase warning volume for misconfigured or noisy webhook senders.
- Stable reason codes are now an operator-facing diagnostic surface; future hook rejection branches should use the same helper and add focused coverage.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- The warning message favors compact grep-friendly key/value fields over verbose prose so it is useful in plain journal/console output.
- Invalid JSON response bodies still include parser text as before, but the new warning line intentionally does not include that parser text to avoid echoing raw body snippets into logs.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change hook auth behavior, status codes, response bodies, mapping selection, dispatch behavior, channel delivery, or session-key policy.
- Does not add a new config option or dependency.
- Does not broaden hook payload logging beyond sanitized metadata.

## Validation

```bash
pnpm test src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
```

Focused tests: 2 files, 36 tests passed.
`pnpm check:changed`: passed core/coreTests lanes.
Final `codex review --base origin/main`: no discrete actionable regressions.
Rebase proof: branch rebased onto `f00a912c25`; focused tests, `check:changed`, and isolated Gateway proof passed after rebase.

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still routes `pnpm docs:spellcheck` through the wrapper that expands an empty `write_flag` array under `set -u`, and the PR body supplies before/after macOS Bash 3.2 terminal proof; I did not have Bash 3.2 locally, so my local check was source-level plus expansion sanity on Bash 5.

target.title: fix(scripts): docs-spellcheck.sh fails on bash 3.2 (macOS); fix the 2 typos it surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` exits with `write_flag[@]: unbound variable` on bash 3.2 (still the default `/bin/bash` on macOS) when invoked without `--write`. The script declares `write_flag=()` then references `"${write_flag[@]}"` later — under `set -u`, bash 3.2 treats `${array[@]}` of an empty array as unbound. Newer bash (≥4.4) handles the same expression correctly, which is why the script ships green on Linux CI runners.
- Why it matters: `pnpm docs:spellcheck` is the supported way to gate docs prose, and contributors on macOS hit this immediately. With the script broken, the actual typo-detection layer is invisible.
- What changed:
  - `scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — switch the array reference to the bash 3.2-safe parameter expansion `${write_flag[@]+"${write_flag[@]}"}` (expands to nothing when empty, to `-w` when set). `--write` mode is unchanged.
  - With the script actually running, codespell surfaces a real typo that was hiding behind the broken script: `overrideable` → `overridable` in `docs/reference/test.md`. (The matching `docs/ci.md` occurrence flagged earlier has since been fixed on `main` independently; only the `docs/reference/test.md` hunk remains.)
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no behavior change for `--write` users on bash 4+; no codespell config edits; no other docs touched.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` exits with `write_flag[@]: unbound variable` on macOS bash 3.2 (default `/bin/bash`), making `pnpm docs:spellcheck` unusable on macOS without first manually switching to a newer shell.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS 26 (Apple Silicon), GNU bash 3.2.57(1)-release (arm64-apple-darwin25), codespell installed via Homebrew.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  bash --version | head -1
  bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh && echo "exit=$?"
  bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write && echo "exit=$?"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal capture on real macOS, post-patch:
  ```
  $ bash --version | head -1
  GNU bash, version 3.2.57(1)-release (arm64-apple-darwin25)

  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh && echo "exit=$?"
  exit=0

  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write && echo "exit=$?"
  exit=0
  ```
  Pre-patch run for comparison (same shell, same checkout, before applying the fix):
  ```
  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh
  scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh: line 30: write_flag[@]: unbound variable
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` no longer aborts on `set -u`; codespell runs to completion and exits 0 in both default and `--write` modes on macOS bash 3.2. Verified that codespell receives an identical argv to the bash-4+ path by running `bash -x scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` and inspecting the resolved `args` array.
- **What was not tested**: I did not exercise the script on a bash 5.x macOS install (Homebrew bash) or on Linux runners, since the bug is bash-3.2-only and the new `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` idiom is the standard recommendation already used elsewhere in the repo (e.g., `scripts/podman/setup.sh:374`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

(No tracking issue — found while investigating docs spelling consistency in #73764.)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: bash 3.2 (Apple's bundled `/bin/bash`) treats `"${empty_array[@]}"` as accessing an unbound variable when `set -u` is active. The script's `args=( ... "${write_flag[@]}" )` line therefore aborts before `codespell` is ever invoked.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there is no macOS-on-bash-3.2 check in CI for the helper scripts themselves. CI runs on Linux Bash 5+, where this bug does not surface.
- Contributing context: typical bash 3.2-safe pattern is `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` — recommended by GNU bash documentation as the conditional-expansion form for arrays under strict mode.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: manual `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` repro on macOS.
- Scenario the test should lock in: invoking the script with no args, on bash 3.2, exits 0 with empty stdout (or codespell findings) — never `unbound variable`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this is a one-line scripting fix; an end-to-end macOS-runner CI job for a docs helper script would dwarf the value of the fix it guards. The simpler ongoing guardrail is the existing `set -u` plus the corrected expansion.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): no automated test, but the codespell command itself is now reachable on bash 3.2.
- If no new test is added, why not: changing from `${empty[@]}` to `${empty[@]+"${empty[@]}"}` is a well-known idiom; a unit test would essentially restate it.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for end users. Internal: contributors on macOS can now run `pnpm docs:spellcheck` (or `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh`) successfully without first switching to bash 4+.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
  bash 3.2 + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh                -> "unbound variable", exit 1
  bash 3.2 + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write        -> works (write_flag is non-empty)
  bash 4+  + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh                -> works
  bash 4+  + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write        -> works

After:
  All four combinations -> work
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No (codespell command line unchanged when `--write` is set; identical argv when no flag)
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26 (Apple Silicon, default `/bin/bash` is GNU bash 3.2.57)
- Runtime/container: N/A
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Fresh checkout of this PR's base.
2. `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — observe `unbound variable` failure.
3. Apply this PR.
4. `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — observe codespell now runs (and after this PR, exits clean).

### Expected

Step 2 fails on bash 3.2; step 4 succeeds.

### Actual

Same as expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — included in the **Real behavior proof** section above and in the commit message.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` on macOS bash 3.2: pre-PR fails, post-PR runs codespell to completion with exit 0.
  - `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write` on macOS bash 3.2: pre-PR works, post-PR still works.
  - `${write_flag[@]+"${write_flag[@]}"}` parameter expansion behavior: confirmed expands to nothing when array is empty, to the array contents when set.
- Edge cases checked: codespell still receives the same args list (verified by visually inspecting the resolved `args` array under both modes).
- What you did not verify: I did not introduce a `behaviour`/`colour` style codespell mapping in `scripts/codespell-dictionary.txt`; that is a separate PR if maintainers want to enforce AmE through codespell directly.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` form is harder to scan at a glance than `"${name[@]}"`.
  - Mitigation: the comment in the surrounding bash file is short and the pattern is the standard recommendation for `set -u` + empty arrays. Not adding inline commentary to keep the diff minimal; happy to add a short comment if reviewers prefer.

target.title: fix(irc): store inbound channel routes as channel:#name and join before send
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes two small IRC channel-routing tweaks:

1. **Store inbound IRC channel reply routes as `channel:#name` instead of `irc:#name`**. IRC sends already accept channel targets as `#name` or `channel:#name`. Persisting a channel reply route as `irc:#name` can later leave the reply path with a target spelling that is less clearly a channel.

2. **Join the channel before sending PRIVMSG for transient IRC sends to channels**. Some IRC servers or channel modes do not accept messages from clients that are not joined to the channel, so joining before PRIVMSG makes one-off channel sends more reliable.

## Changes

- `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts`: Changed `From` and `OriginatingTo` fields for group messages to use `channel:#{target}` prefix instead of `irc:channel:{target}`.
- `extensions/irc/src/send.ts`: Added channel JOIN before PRIVMSG in the transient-client send path, guarded by target starts with `#` or `&`.
- `extensions/irc/src/inbound.behavior.test.ts`: Added test verifying group messages use `channel:#` prefix in `From` and `OriginatingTo` fields.

## Verification

Targeted test run (from the issue description):

```
pnpm test extensions/irc/src/send.test.ts extensions/irc/src/inbound.behavior.test.ts

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       11 passed (11)
```

Summary

- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping, plus a changelog fix entry for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 106 tests passed

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node 22 with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not run for this source-level fix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The new CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 106 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff check, and the branch was rebased onto current `main` with an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run before opening this PR; the proof is focused source-level regression coverage for the broken dependency lookup path.

Fixes #84952

## Summary

Skips wildcard default model allowlist entries while building concrete model catalog metadata and configured `models list` rows.

Entries such as `openai-codex/*` are valid allowlist selectors, but they are not concrete model ids and do not have per-model alias metadata to resolve or display as configured rows. target.title: fix(cron): use original config for isolated delivery secretRef resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fix cron isolated-agent delivery dispatch to use the original config (carrying runtime-resolved secretRefs) for credential-sensitive channel operations, instead of the agent-defaults config that strips them.

Closes #86545

## Problem

When a cron job runs in isolated mode, `dispatchCronDelivery` receives two config objects:

- `params.cfg`: the original config with runtime-resolved `secretRefs` needed for channel authentication
- `params.cfgWithAgentDefaults`: a spread copy with agent defaults applied, but without the runtime-resolved secret references

Three credential-sensitive calls used `params.cfgWithAgentDefaults`:

1. `resolveDirectCronDeliverySessionKey` (line 912): resolves the delivery session key, which requires channel credentials
2. `buildOutboundSessionContext` (line 918): builds the outbound session context for channel delivery
3. `sendDurableMessageBatch` (line 950): sends the actual message batch to the channel (e.g., Discord)

All three fail to resolve channel credentials because the spread config does not carry the runtime-resolved `secretRef` bindings. The remaining 5 uses of `cfgWithAgentDefaults` in the same function correctly need agent-level settings (identity resolution, TTS, awareness, transcript mirror, store path) and are left unchanged.

## Change

`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`: changed 3 lines from `params.cfgWithAgentDefaults` to `params.cfg` for the three credential-sensitive calls listed above.

## Verification

- All check gates pass (typecheck, conflict markers, dependency pins, sidecar loader, etc.); the only lint failure is a pre-existing oxlint finding in an unrelated file
- Traced the config construction in `run.ts:514` to confirm the shallow spread does not propagate runtime-resolved secret reference bindings
- Audited all 8 `cfgWithAgentDefaults` usages in the delivery function; the 5 unchanged ones need agent settings, not credentials

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cron isolated jobs fail Discord (and other channel) delivery because secretRefs are not resolved when the delivery dispatch uses the agent-defaults config instead of the original config
Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22, openclaw source checkout at latest main (639107b7db)
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Traced the config object lifecycle from entry to delivery. In `run.ts:514`, `cfgWithAgentDefaults` is constructed via:

```
const cfgWithAgentDefaults: OpenClawConfig = {
    ...input.cfg,
    agents: Object.assign({}, input.cfg.agents, { defaults: agentCfg }),
};
```

This shallow spread copies top-level properties but does not propagate runtime-attached secretRef resolution state. The original `input.cfg` (passed through as `params.cfg` at line 1036) retains the resolved references. After the patch, the three delivery calls (`resolveDirectCronDeliverySessionKey`, `buildOutboundSessionContext`, `sendDurableMessageBatch`) receive `params.cfg` instead of `params.cfgWithAgentDefaults`, giving them access to the channel credential bindings.

Evidence after fix: The config routing is now split by concern: credential-sensitive channel delivery calls use `params.cfg` (original config with secretRef bindings), while agent-settings calls (identity, TTS, awareness, transcript mirror, store path) continue using `params.cfgWithAgentDefaults`. This matches the pattern used by the non-isolated agent run path where delivery functions receive the original config, not the agent-defaults overlay.

```
// Before (broken): delivery calls use config without secretRef bindings
resolveDirectCronDeliverySessionKey({ cfg: params.cfgWithAgentDefaults, ... })
buildOutboundSessionContext({ cfg: params.cfgWithAgentDefaults, ... })
sendDurableMessageBatch({ cfg: params.cfgWithAgentDefaults, ... })

// After (fixed): delivery calls use original config with secretRef bindings
resolveDirectCronDeliverySessionKey({ cfg: params.cfg, ... })
buildOutboundSessionContext({ cfg: params.cfg, ... })
sendDurableMessageBatch({ cfg: params.cfg, ... })
```

Observed result after fix: All gates pass. The three credential-sensitive calls now receive the config object that carries runtime-resolved secret references, consistent with how non-isolated delivery paths handle channel authentication.
What was not tested: Live Discord delivery with a real cron isolated job was not tested (requires configured channel credentials and a running gateway with scheduled cron jobs). The fix is a 3-line config routing change with no logic changes.

target.title: fix(auth): cooldown inline api key billing failures
target.body:
## Summary
- track provider-level usage/backoff state for inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials without creating synthetic auth profiles
- skip temporarily disabled inline API keys during auth resolution and availability checks
- mark inline API keys on provider auth/billing failures so repeated 402s do not immediately retry the same exhausted key

Closes #39807.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials now record provider auth/billing failures in usage state, stop immediate reuse during cooldown, and become available again after the cooldown expires without creating a synthetic auth profile.
- **Environment tested**: Local Linux workstation on PR branch `fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown` at `61ac6e93eac913fee891d3d63262518e08185c9d`, using Node/tsx against the production auth profile store, usage helpers, and model auth resolution helpers.
- **Steps run after the patch**: Ran a Node terminal probe that created a temporary agent directory, configured a dummy inline provider API key, resolved availability before failure, called `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)` with reason `billing`, checked availability/resolution during cooldown, then simulated cooldown expiry and resolved the inline key again.
- **Evidence**:

```text
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=real-proof-inline-billing profile=sha256:f68577404b84 provider=demo-local reason=billing window=disabled reused=false
{
  "behavior": "inline provider apiKey billing failure disables only that inline key and blocks immediate reuse",
  "beforeFailure": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  },
  "recordedFailure": {
    "usageId": "inline-api-key:demo-local",
    "profileCreated": false,
    "disabledReason": "billing",
    "disabledUntilIsFuture": true
  },
  "duringCooldown": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": false,
    "blocked": true,
    "blockedMessage": "Inline API key for provider \"demo-local\" is temporarily disabled after a provider auth/billing failure. Retry after about 300 minutes, or switch to a different auth profile/API key."
  },
  "afterCooldownExpiry": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result**: Before the billing failure, the inline key is available and resolves from `models.json`. After `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)`, `usageStats["inline-api-key:demo-local"]` records `disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil` is in the future, no synthetic profile is created, `hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...)` returns false, and `resolveApiKeyForProvider(...)` blocks immediate reuse with the temporary-disabled error. After simulated cooldown expiry, the same inline key becomes available and resolves from `models.json` again.
- **Not tested**: A live paid provider 402 against a real provider account; this avoids intentionally causing a real billing failure while still exercising the production failure-marking and auth-resolution path with a redacted dummy inline key.

## Validation

## Additional real behavior proof

- **Environment tested**: local checkout after merging upstream/main into fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown at ce378baab4.
- **Evidence after fix**: added runner-path coverage that simulates an inline models.providers.openai.apiKey billing failure with no auth profile and verifies auth-state.json records inline-api-key:openai with disabledReason: "billing" and a future disabledUntil.
- **Validation after fix**:

Provider availability also reports env-backed inline keys unavailable while the inline cooldown window is active.

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
## Problem

Four code paths in `scripts/install.sh` download remote scripts via `download_file` and execute them with `bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

Affected paths:
1. `run_remote_bash` (Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`)
2. NodeSource apt/deb setup script (`deb.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
3. NodeSource dnf/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
4. NodeSource yum/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)

Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

### Layer 1: Download failure guards

Every download-then-execute path now aborts immediately when `download_file` (or its `run_quiet_step` wrapper) fails:

- `run_remote_bash`: `download_file "$url" "$tmp" || return 1`
- All three NodeSource paths: `run_quiet_step ... download_file ... || return 1`

Without these guards, `run_quiet_step`'s `if "$@"` wrapper suppresses `set -e`, so a failed `download_file` that left a partial file on disk would fall through to validation and execution. This addresses the [P2] finding from ClawSweeper review.

### Layer 2: Structural validation

Extract a reusable `validate_downloaded_script()` function and apply it to all four download-then-execute paths. The validation:
1. Rejects empty files (`[[ ! -s "$file" ]]`)
2. Rejects files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`) using raw byte check (`od -An -tx1 -N2`)
3. Sanitizes the first-line diagnostic before logging: strips control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F, 0x7F, 0x80-0x9F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d` and escapes backslashes so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because these setup scripts update frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` and three NodeSource paths executed downloaded content unconditionally. Two separate failure modes: (1) `download_file` failure was not checked, so a failed curl/wget leaving a partial shebang-prefixed file would pass validation and reach `/bin/bash`; (2) no structural validation existed, so HTML error pages, empty files, and binary blobs would be executed as scripts.

Real environment tested: macOS (bash 3.2) and Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2, bash 5.x). The real patched functions were extracted from `scripts/install.sh` and exercised against local HTTP/HTTPS fixtures.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
-- GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads --
  PASS  empty file  -> rejected
  PASS  HTML error   -> rejected
  PASS  JSON error   -> rejected
  PASS  binary blob  -> rejected
  PASS  C1 escape    -> rejected
  PASS  NUL prefix   -> rejected (raw byte check caught NUL before #!)
  PASS  C1 diagnostic -> C1 bytes stripped from error output
  PASS  valid script (#!bash)     -> accepted
  PASS  valid script (#!env bash) -> accepted
  PASS  partial download (valid shebang) -> accepted by validation alone
  PASS  failed download (partial) -> pipeline rejected before validation

-- RED: no validation lets bad downloads through --
  PASS  empty file  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  HTML error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  JSON error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  binary blob  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 15 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed
```

The two new partial-download tests (lines 10-11 in GREEN) prove the download_file failure guard is essential:
- Test 10: `partial.sh` has a valid `#!/bin/bash` shebang, so `validate_downloaded_script` alone ACCEPTS it
- Test 11: The full pipeline with a failing downloader REJECTS before reaching validation, because `download_file ... || return 1` exits early

Observed result after fix: All 15 harness tests pass. Download failure guards prevent partial files from reaching validation. Structural validation catches HTML errors, empty files, binary blobs, C1 escape attacks, and NUL-prefixed files. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against terminal injection.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against local HTTP and HTTPS servers instead).

target.title: fix(qa): reduce WhatsApp live RTT setup cost
target.body:
Summary
- Register WhatsApp through a narrow runtime setter entrypoint so gateway startup no longer imports the broad WhatsApp runtime barrel before live message handling.
- Add WhatsApp live QA retry handling for logged-out Convex credentials; stale leases stay held while the lane tries alternate pooled credentials.
- Add a maintainer-role workflow input for WhatsApp Convex leases, and switch QA gateway-child readiness from `config.get` to lightweight `health` RPC to avoid loading the full config schema during startup checks.
- Keep remote changed gates on the Corepack pnpm shim in Blacksmith runner shells so Testbox `pnpm check:changed` is stable even when launched directly through Crabbox.
- Keep the existing WhatsApp RTT instrumentation, heap snapshots, gateway traces, and changelog coverage on this branch.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/whatsapp/index.ts extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/runtime-setter-api.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json scripts/check-changed.mjs test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate: `tbx_01ks94abkaf4rjwh7hemswwb6t`, Actions run `26318665070`, `pnpm check:changed` at `8f1403f35b3` passed.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate: `tbx_01ks9amfdrepmf5w8bt3yt4ajd`, Actions run `26321093857`, `corepack pnpm check:changed` at `1c6acf13de8` passed.

Live evidence
- `26315828755` at `c2bad2a4099`: WhatsApp job failed before RTT; CI Convex credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac` was logged out (`401 Unauthorized`).
- `26316577807` at `9897bcfe79a`: maintainer role failed before RTT because `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_MAINTAINER` is not configured.
- `26317166666` at `57a09eee274`: retry path held the logged-out CI credential and attempted another lease; broker exhausted after 360000ms, proving the CI WhatsApp pool currently has no alternate healthy credential.
- `26321264487` at `1c6acf13de8`: latest WhatsApp artifact `qa-live-whatsapp-26321264487-1` shows the same CI credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac`, logged out, then Convex credential pool exhaustion after 360000ms with no observed messages.
- Repo secret listing confirms `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_CI` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SITE_URL` exist, but `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_MAINTAINER` is absent.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA now avoids one eager runtime import path, reports stale Convex WhatsApp credentials without silently reacquiring the same row, skips full config-schema work for gateway-child RPC readiness, preserves retry auth directories across successful alternate credential startup, and keeps direct Crabbox/Testbox changed gates off stale runner pnpm shims.
Real environment tested: GitHub Actions live WhatsApp Convex QA on Blacksmith runners, Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate, plus targeted local Vitest/oxlint/diff checks in the branch worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `qa-live-transports-convex.yml` with `ref=whatsapp-rtt-leak`, `whatsapp_scenario=whatsapp-canary`, `whatsapp_credential_role=ci`, `matrix_profile=fast`; Testbox-through-Crabbox `corepack pnpm check:changed` on `whatsapp-rtt-leak` after deepening `origin/main`.
Evidence after fix: stale credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac` is identified and held; alternate acquisition exhausts, so current live RTT proof is blocked by credential pool state rather than an unclassified harness failure; `pnpm check:changed` is green at `1c6acf13de8`.
Observed result after fix: branch improves startup/QA hygiene and credential diagnosis, but it does not yet prove the WhatsApp RTT target; the only CI WhatsApp credential is logged out.
What was not tested: a successful live WhatsApp RTT run after `1c6acf13de8`; current Convex credential state blocks that proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid OpenAI realtime double greeting
target.body:
Fixes #85846

## Summary
- Avoid the voice-call plugin's duplicate OpenAI realtime greeting by not calling `triggerGreeting()` for OpenAI when server VAD will auto-create the first response.
- Preserve the configured outbound initial message for OpenAI by passing the built greeting instructions into the OpenAI realtime session instructions.
- Keep explicit `triggerGreeting()` initial greetings enabled for non-OpenAI realtime providers such as Google.
- Add focused realtime handler regressions for no-message, OpenAI initial-message, and non-OpenAI initial-message paths.

## Why
OpenAI realtime server VAD defaults to `create_response: true`. For outbound voice calls with an initial message, the telephony handler also queued an explicit greeting via `triggerGreeting()`, so OpenAI could create two back-to-back responses on the first caller turn. The repair now gives OpenAI the initial message once through session instructions and leaves the explicit greeting trigger disabled for that provider.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 20 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`

Note: commit is SSH-signed locally and verified with `git log --show-signature -1` after rebasing onto current `main`. No live Twilio/OpenAI call was run in this environment.

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Keep CLI lazy outbound dependency routing from treating Discord voice helper keys as normal channel adapters.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping, plus a changelog fix entry for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 106 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node 22 with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not run for this source-level fix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The new CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 106 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff check, and the branch was rebased onto current `main` with an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run before opening this PR; the proof is focused source-level regression coverage for the broken dependency lookup path.

Fixes #84952
