target.title: fix(security): bump qs from 6.14.2 to 6.15.2 (GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26)
target.body:
fix(security): bump qs from 6.14.2 to 6.15.2 to resolve GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26

## Summary
`openclaw@2026.5.25` ships an `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that vendors `qs@6.14.2`, which has a known moderate DoS vulnerability ([GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26)). Because shrinkwrap is authoritative, consumers cannot override this dependency via `package.json` overrides or `npm audit fix`.

## Changes
- Add `"qs": "6.15.2"` to `package.json` `overrides`
- Add `qs: 6.15.2` to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` `overrides`
- Update `npm-shrinkwrap.json` to lock `qs@6.15.2` with correct resolved URL and integrity hash
- Update `pnpm-lock.yaml` to lock `qs@6.15.2` via `pnpm install`

## Real behavior proof

### 1. Behavior or issue addressed
`qs@6.14.2` crashes with `TypeError` on null/undefined entries in `arrayFormat: 'comma'` arrays when `encodeValuesOnly` is set. This is a moderate severity DoS vector. The shrinkwrap file locks this vulnerable version, preventing consumers from fixing it via `npm audit fix`.

### 2. Real environment tested
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS
- Node: v24.14.1
- npm: 11.11.0
- pnpm: available in PATH
- openclaw commit: `b7b29bb281ad118be6de70c42d1e36891f1c5e95`
- Date: 2026-05-26

### 3. Reproduction (before fix)

**Terminal output — live run on commit before fix:**
```
$ cd /tmp/doncicx-openclaw && npm audit --omit=dev

qs  < 6.15.2
Severity: moderate
qs before 6.15.2 allows stack overflow (DoS)
https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26
Package: qs
Patched in: >=6.15.2
Dependency of: openclaw
Path: openclaw > express > qs
More info: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26

found 1 moderate severity vulnerability
```

### 4. Fix verification (after fix)

**Terminal output — live run after applying the fix:**
```
$ cd /tmp/doncicx-openclaw && npm audit --omit=dev
found 0 vulnerabilities
```

**Verification that qs@6.15.2 is locked:**
```
$ grep -A2 '"node_modules/qs"' npm-shrinkwrap.json
    "node_modules/qs": {
      "version": "6.15.2",
      "resolved": "https://registry.npmjs.org/qs/-/qs-6.15.2.tgz",
      "integrity": "sha512-Rzq0KEyX/w/tEybncDgdkZrJgVUsUMk3xjh3t5bv3S1HTAtg+uOYt72+ZfwiQwKdysThkTBdL/rTi6HDmX9Ddw=="
```

### 5. Regression test
- `npm audit --omit=dev` passes with 0 vulnerabilities
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` updated via `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile` — all `qs` entries now show `6.15.2`
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` override aligned to `6.15.2`
- No functional changes to openclaw code

### 6. Test results
`npm audit` passes with 0 vulnerabilities. All lockfiles (`npm-shrinkwrap.json` + `pnpm-lock.yaml`) are consistent at `qs@6.15.2`.

## Impact
- Affected: all consumers installing `openclaw` via npm
- Severity: Moderate (DoS)
- Backwards compatibility: yes — `qs@6.15.2` is a patch release in the same semver line

Refs #86457

target.title: fix(agents): strip markdown code spans from IDENTITY.md values and labels
target.body:
## Summary

- `parseIdentityMarkdown` strips `*` and `_` from start/end of values but not backticks, so an avatar declared as `` - **Avatar:** `avatars/foo.png` `` is parsed as the literal string `` `avatars/foo.png` ``. `path.extname` then returns `` .png` ``, which fails `isSupportedLocalAvatarExtension`, and the Control UI surfaces "Unsupported image type".
- Extend the formatting-strip character class on both the value and the label to include backticks (markdown code spans), aligned with the existing `*`/`_` handling. Also include `\s` in the value-side strip class so the leading `**` of a wrapped label and the leading `` ` `` of a wrapped value, separated by a space, are stripped together in one pass.
- Plain text values continue to work exactly as before.
- Out of scope: other markdown formatting (`~`, links `[text](url)`, etc.). They can be addressed in follow-ups if needed; this PR fixes the observed, deterministic break.
- Success: `IDENTITY.md` lines that wrap values or labels in markdown code spans parse to the same identity object as the unwrapped equivalent.
- Reviewers should focus on the regex char class change and on the new test cases (which also pin the existing placeholder behavior under code-span wrapping).

## Linked context

Closes #86594

Related #

Not requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: avatar declared with `` - **Avatar:** `avatars/<name>.png` `` in `IDENTITY.md` renders as "Unsupported image type" in the Control UI.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 installed via pnpm global, gateway running as a systemd user unit on `127.0.0.1:18789`, ollama/qwen3 model, Caddy in front. Workspace at `~/.openclaw/workspace`, avatar PNG at `~/.openclaw/workspace/avatars/ksorayn.png` (12840 bytes, valid PNG, RGBA 1360x800).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  Run the gateway's own modules against the affected file, with the patched parser source linked in:

  ```sh
  node --input-type=module -e "
    import { parseIdentityMarkdown } from './src/agents/identity-file.ts';
    import { isSupportedLocalAvatarExtension, isPathWithinRoot } from './src/shared/avatar-policy.ts';
    import fs from 'node:fs';
    import path from 'node:path';
    const ws = '<workspace>';
    for (const src of [
      '- **Avatar:** \`avatars/ksorayn.png\`',
      '- **Avatar:** avatars/ksorayn.png',
      '- \`Avatar\`: \`avatars/ksorayn.png\`',
      '- **\`Avatar\`**: \`avatars/ksorayn.png\`',
    ]) {
      const id = parseIdentityMarkdown(src);
      const real = fs.realpathSync(path.resolve(ws, id.avatar));
      console.log(src, '->', JSON.stringify(id.avatar), 'ok=', isSupportedLocalAvatarExtension(real) && isPathWithinRoot(ws, real));
    }
  "
  ```

- Evidence after fix: terminal output of `node` running the script above against the patched parser, copied live from the gateway host:

  ```
  - **Avatar:** `avatars/ksorayn.png`              -> "avatars/ksorayn.png" ok= true
  - **Avatar:** avatars/ksorayn.png                -> "avatars/ksorayn.png" ok= true
  - `Avatar`: `avatars/ksorayn.png`                -> "avatars/ksorayn.png" ok= true
  - **`Avatar`**: `avatars/ksorayn.png`            -> "avatars/ksorayn.png" ok= true
  ```

  Before the fix, the same `node` script against the shipped 2026.5.22 dist returned the buggy line below; this is the live terminal output that motivated the patch:

  ```
  - **Avatar:** `avatars/ksorayn.png`              -> "`avatars/ksorayn.png`" ok= false  (extname = ".png`")
  ```

- Observed result after fix: in the live `openclaw` gateway running on `127.0.0.1:18789`, the avatar slot in the Control UI renders the PNG (visually verified in the browser); the gateway no longer reports `unsupported_extension` for the wrapped path in its runtime log.
- What was not tested: rendering with other channels (Telegram, Slack, Discord) and macOS app build — the change is in shared parser code with no channel-specific code paths, so unaffected by channel rendering.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none significant; reproducible from the failing `IDENTITY.md` alone.
- Before evidence (optional): the original Control UI tooltip showed `` `avatars/ksorayn.png` Unsupported image type `` on the same file before the fix.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```
pnpm install
pnpm test src/agents/identity-file.test.ts   # 6/6 pass (4 existing + 2 new)
pnpm check:changed                            # typecheck core + tests, lint, guards, cycles, deps — all green
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/identity-file.ts src/agents/identity-file.test.ts
```

Regression coverage added in `src/agents/identity-file.test.ts`:

- `strips markdown code spans from values and labels` — covers value-only, label-only, and combined `**`+`` ` `` wrapping.
- `still treats code-span-wrapped template placeholders as placeholders` — pins existing behavior so a wrapped placeholder is not promoted to a real value.

What failed before this fix: the new `strips markdown code spans...` test fails on unpatched `main` (value retains backticks).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change: **No** for existing valid `IDENTITY.md` files (no backticks). **Yes (positive)** for files that wrap values/labels in markdown code spans — they now parse instead of silently failing.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior: **No**. No new config keys, no schema change, no migration needed.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior: **No**. The parser is pure string handling; the existing avatar policy (path-within-root, max bytes, allowed extensions) is untouched and still enforces all guards.
- Highest-risk area: the value-side strip now also consumes leading/trailing whitespace as part of the formatting strip. A pre-existing `.trim()` already removed edge whitespace, so this is functionally equivalent for current well-formed values — but it does collapse some edge cases (e.g., `"** value"` ⇒ `"value"` directly without the intermediate space).
- Mitigation: new tests cover both wrapped and unwrapped variants; the placeholder regression test confirms placeholder detection still fires; `pnpm check:changed` confirms the change is contained to the agents lane with no cycle/type/lint regression.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: maintainer triage. CI will run on push.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: Fix plugin packaging recovery hints
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?
- Fixes the remaining #80490 misdirection where plugin packaging failures could still lead startup/config validation users toward the generic `openclaw doctor --fix` recovery hint.

Why does this matter now?
- ClawSweeper found that installed source-only TypeScript plugin packages should remain blocked unless publishers ship compiled JavaScript, but the recovery hint still implied a local doctor repair.

What is the intended outcome?
- Plugin packaging / compiled-runtime-output failures keep the packaging diagnostic and show update/reinstall-after-publisher-fix or disable/uninstall guidance instead of `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Normal invalid config failures still show the doctor recovery hint.

What is intentionally out of scope?
- No doctor auto-build path for arbitrary third-party plugin source.
- No plugin build-system changes and no changelog edits.

What does success look like?
- Users with a source-only installed plugin package see packaging-specific remediation in config validation and gateway startup, without losing doctor guidance for unrelated invalid config cases.

What should reviewers focus on?
- The classifier in `src/config/recovery-policy.ts`, especially that it only suppresses doctor guidance for compiled-runtime-output packaging diagnostics and same-plugin `plugin not found` fallout.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #80490

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related: ClawSweeper guidance in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80490#issuecomment-4416918935

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

ClawSweeper guidance requested a focused repair that corrects startup/config guidance without auto-building third-party plugin source.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: source-only installed plugin package referenced from config should fail with plugin packaging guidance, not `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Real environment tested: Crabbox local-container Linux proof, disposable Docker image `openclaw-issue-80490-proof:local`, provider `local-container`, lease `cbx_0b5f611fb5a6`, slug `silver-shrimp`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a temporary Linux state dir with an installed `@openclaw/source-only-pack` package whose manifest points at `./index.ts` and no compiled JS, wrote config with `plugins.slots.memory="source-only-pack"`, then ran `pnpm openclaw config validate` and `timeout 30s pnpm openclaw gateway run`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
CONFIG_STATUS=1
OpenClaw config is invalid: /tmp/openclaw-issue-80490-state/openclaw.json
  × plugins.slots.memory: plugin not found: source-only-pack

This is a plugin packaging issue, not a local config problem.
Update or reinstall the plugin after the publisher ships compiled JavaScript, or disable/uninstall the plugin until then.
Inspect with openclaw config validate.

GATEWAY_STATUS=1
Gateway failed to start: Invalid config at /tmp/openclaw-issue-80490-state/openclaw.json.
plugins.slots.memory: plugin not found: source-only-pack
This is a plugin packaging issue, not a local config problem.
Update or reinstall the plugin after the publisher ships compiled JavaScript, or disable/uninstall the plugin until then.. Run openclaw gateway status --deep for diagnostics.
REAL_PROOF_OK issue-80490 plugin packaging startup/config validation hints
```

- Observed result after fix: both real CLI entrypoints failed for the source-only installed plugin package with packaging-specific remediation; grep checks confirmed `openclaw doctor --fix` was absent from both outputs.
- What was not tested: live VPS, live channels, provider accounts, or real third-party plugin registry state.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the normal Crabbox local-container sync path failed on this Windows host because rsync was unavailable in the sync shell, and AWS Crabbox was blocked by missing AWS credentials. I used Crabbox local-container with `--no-sync` and a disposable Docker image containing the patched checkout to keep the proof in a real Linux/container lane without touching live runtime state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper source-level repro in #80490 showed gateway startup still appended `formatInvalidConfigRecoveryHint()` for this failure shape before the patch.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

target.title: fix(vllm): wire configured thinking params
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- vLLM thinking controls can now be model-aware instead of exposing a generic multi-level ladder for models that only support binary thinking.
- Configured Qwen thinking params now reach the model catalog used by `/think` and provider policy resolution, including allowlist-only `agents.defaults.models` entries.
- The vLLM stream wrapper now reads the same configured Qwen thinking format as the UI/session policy path.

Why does this matter now?

- This follows the model-aware thinking PRD in #86099 and fixes the inconsistency where configured vLLM/Qwen params could affect streaming while `/think` still showed unsupported levels.

What is the intended outcome?

- Configured vLLM Qwen models expose persistent binary `off`/`on` thinking choices, and those choices map to the correct vLLM request fields.
- Nemotron 3 vLLM reasoning models keep the existing binary profile behavior.
- Runtime-only model params are not exposed through `models.list` responses.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Changing non-vLLM provider thinking semantics.
- Changing Pi OpenAI harness behavior beyond passing catalog `params`/`compat` into provider thinking policy context.

What does success look like?

- Provider-declared and allowlist-only model params both resolve into the configured catalog.
- `/think` choices and vLLM stream request patching agree for configured Qwen thinking formats.
- Focused model-selection, thinking-policy, vLLM stream, gateway model-list, docs, diff, and build gates pass.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The catalog merge/synthesis path in `src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts`.
- The vLLM-owned binary thinking profile and stream patching contract in `extensions/vllm/thinking-policy.ts` and `extensions/vllm/stream.ts`.
- The `models.list` params redaction in `src/gateway/server-methods/models.ts`.
- The additive provider thinking context docs in `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #86099

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #72329
Related #79677
Related #82768

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested through the vLLM/OpenClaw thinking-mode workstream and captured in #86099.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: vLLM/Qwen thinking configuration can now drive both `/think` choices and stream request patching through the same configured model params/compat seam.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw CLI built from this PR branch, isolated temp config/state, and live k3s vLLM router at `http://127.0.0.1:31080/v1` with bearer token redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:31080/health`
  - `curl -sS -H 'Authorization: Bearer [redacted]' http://127.0.0.1:31080/v1/models`
  - `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/state pnpm openclaw models list --provider vllm --json`
  - `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/state pnpm openclaw infer model run --local --model vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 --prompt 'Reply exactly: VLLM_THINK_OK' --thinking off --json`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  $ curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:31080/health
  200

  $ curl -sS -H 'Authorization: Bearer [redacted]' http://127.0.0.1:31080/v1/models
  {"object":"list","data":[{"id":"qwen3-vl-embedding-2b","object":"model","created":1779664611,"owned_by":"vllm","root":null,"parent":null},{"id":"qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4","object":"model","created":1779664611,"owned_by":"vllm","root":null,"parent":null}]}

  $ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/state pnpm openclaw models list --provider vllm --json
  {
    "count": 1,
    "models": [
      {
        "key": "vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4",
        "name": "qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4",
        "input": "text",
        "contextWindow": 200000,
        "local": false,
        "available": true,
        "tags": [
          "default",
          "configured",
          "alias:Qwen binary thinking"
        ],
        "missing": false
      }
    ]
  }

  $ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-vllm-think-proof/state pnpm openclaw infer model run --local --model vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 --prompt 'Reply exactly: VLLM_THINK_OK' --thinking off --json
  {
    "ok": true,
    "capability": "model.run",
    "transport": "local",
    "provider": "vllm",
    "model": "qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4",
    "attempts": [],
    "outputs": [
      {
        "text": "VLLM_THINK_OK",
        "mediaUrl": null
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the isolated config's allowlist-only vLLM/Qwen model appears in the OpenClaw model list without leaking `params`, and a real OpenClaw local inference run reaches the live vLLM Qwen model with `--thinking off` and returns the requested output.
- What was not tested: a separate Nemotron live deployment was not available in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: live proof used the currently running local k3s vLLM Qwen deployment; request payload internals were validated by focused unit coverage rather than packet/log capture.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): review finding reproduced the gap where allowlist-only `agents.defaults.models["vllm/Qwen/Qwen3-8B"].params` did not populate the configured catalog used by thinking policy resolution.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/vllm/provider-policy-api.test.ts extensions/vllm/stream.test.ts extensions/vllm/provider-discovery.contract.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm lint:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- After final rebase to `ec168fa2bdda15be7430f41cb7ef0b610b6e68bf`:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/vllm/provider-policy-api.test.ts extensions/vllm/stream.test.ts extensions/vllm/provider-discovery.contract.test.ts --reporter dot`
  - `git diff --check`
- Live proof commands listed in the real behavior proof section.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- vLLM provider thinking policy tests for Qwen params/compat and Nemotron binary profiles.
- vLLM stream tests for params/compat/model params and Qwen patching when reasoning metadata is absent but not explicitly disabled.
- Model-selection tests for params merging, allowlist-only synthetic catalog entries, manifest-normalized provider metadata, and preserving discovered catalog names.
- Auto-reply thinking tests for passing catalog params/compat into provider policy hooks.
- Gateway models tests for redacting runtime params from `models.list` responses.
- SDK provider docs for the additive `resolveThinkingProfile(ctx)` context fields.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A review pass identified that allowlist-only `agents.defaults.models` params did not populate `buildConfiguredModelCatalog`, so `/think` and provider policy resolution could diverge from stream behavior.
- PR CI initially failed `check-lint`, `check-test-types`, and bundled-extension lint; the branch now includes fixes for those local reproductions.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added for each changed behavior path.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- Model catalog metadata merging in `src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts`, because it feeds model visibility and thinking policy context.
- The additive plugin-facing provider thinking context fields (`compat` and `params`).

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change preserves existing provider-declared catalog behavior, adds exact tests for allowlist-only params and manifest-normalized provider rows, redacts params from public `models.list` output, and documents the new provider SDK context contract.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review/merge decision.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- GitHub status checks are green on `4370cb0e12ecdcd94c61ee2d47b9176571c10e9a`; PR merge state is clean.
- ClawSweeper accepted a re-review command for `4370cb0e12ecdcd94c61ee2d47b9176571c10e9a`; the existing durable review is already positive and says the remaining decision is maintainer approval of the additive provider thinking context API shape.
- Maintainer review is needed for the additive provider thinking context API shape.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed the P2 review finding that per-model params from `agents.defaults.models` were not propagated when no provider row existed.
- Addressed the follow-up reviewloop finding that manifest-normalized provider metadata needed to use one resolved manifest plugin snapshot consistently.
- Addressed ClawSweeper's P2 request to document the new provider thinking context fields.
- Replaced the earlier source-level-only PR proof with live OpenClaw + live vLLM proof.

target.title: fix(agents): publish pi's owned writes via onMessagePersisted (#86572)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` in vanilla openclaw caused by pi's stream iteration writing via direct `appendFileSync` between `releaseForPrompt` and the next external-hook lock acquisition. The lane's own writes are now recorded in the existing `ownedSessionFileWrites` machinery and accepted via `assertSessionFileFence`'s owned-write match path, while genuine external mutations still trip the fence.

Closes #86572.

## Redesign note (force-pushed)

This PR was re-designed after ClawSweeper review on the original commit (`91ba92c9ab`). The earlier `refreshBeforeLock` shim used a blanket `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite()` right before every external-hook lock acquisition. That approach unconditionally snapped the current file fingerprint into the fence — including for genuine external mutations — which silently weakened takeover detection. The current commit (`d824be0298`) routes pi's writes through the existing trust-gated owned-write machinery instead.

## Root cause

`_handleAgentEvent` → `sessionManager.appendMessage` → `_persist` uses synchronous `appendFileSync` directly. OpenClaw normally catches these writes via the `onMessagePersisted` callback. When pi fires two streaming events in quick succession:

1. Write N fires (file: F0 → F1). `onMessagePersisted` for N runs synchronously after the appendFileSync.
2. Write N+1 fires (file: F1 → F2). `onMessagePersisted` for N+1 runs.
3. `beforeToolCall` fires between writes. `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` wraps it with `withSessionWriteLock` → `assertSessionFileFence` reads current fingerprint = F2, compares against `fenceFingerprint` = F0 (still pre-prompt-release), and trips because the prior `onMessagePersisted` callbacks only called `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite()` which updates the local fence but doesn't record the writes in the process-shared `ownedSessionFileWrites` map.

## Fix

Add a new `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController.publishOwnedPostMessageWrite()` method that records pi's writes as **owned** (not just refreshes the fence) and gate it on `trustedSessionFileStates`:

```ts
publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(): void {
  if (takeoverDetected || !fenceFingerprint) return;
  const current = readSessionFileFingerprintSync(params.lockOptions.sessionFile);
  if (sameSessionFileFingerprint(fenceFingerprint, current)) return;
  // Trust gate: only publish if the prior fence was set at releaseForPrompt
  // time (where trustSessionFileState() marks the fingerprint trusted).
  if (!isTrustedSessionFileState(sessionFileFenceKey, fenceFingerprint)) return;
  const generation = recordOwnedSessionFileWrite(sessionFileFenceKey, current);
  if (fenceActive) {
    fenceFingerprint = current;
    fenceSnapshot = { fingerprint: current };
    fenceGeneration = generation;
  }
}
```

Wire it from `runEmbeddedAttempt`'s `onMessagePersisted` callback (the existing pi `_persist` hook point):

```ts
onMessagePersisted: () => {
  sessionLockController.publishOwnedPostMessageWrite();
},
```

Revert `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock`'s `refreshBeforeLock` parameter and the shared `waitBeforeLock` closure — each `installLockableFunction` goes back to `waitBeforeLock: () => waitForSessionEventQueue(session)`.

### Why this addresses the bot's concern

ClawSweeper required: *"only advance the fence for writes proven to be this controller's own pi/session-manager persistence, and add a negative test for an external same-file append before a hook lock."*

- **Proven owned writes:** Only the `onMessagePersisted` callback path calls `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite`. That callback only fires when this lane's `sessionManager.appendMessage` succeeds — proven this-controller's-own persistence.
- **Trust gate:** The new method checks `isTrustedSessionFileState(sessionFileFenceKey, fenceFingerprint)` before recording. `trustedSessionFileStates` is populated at `releaseForPrompt` time by `trustSessionFileState()` (existing function). External mutations between `releaseForPrompt` and the next `onMessagePersisted` cannot taint the trusted state because the prior fence fingerprint is what's checked, not the current file state.
- **Negative test included:** `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — fail-closed invariant preserved.

I/O cost: one `statSync` per pi message persistence (same path as the previous `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite()` call it replaces). No per-token cost. No new lock acquisition.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Eliminates spurious `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on the lane's own pi-driven writes when an external hook fires after rapid pi writes. The takeover fence still trips on genuine external mutations.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout (macOS 15.5, Node 22.19+) at the patched branch HEAD `d824be0298`, branched off `3eb06e305e` (current upstream main). The new regression tests exercise the production `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` + `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` machinery against real temp session JSONL files via `fs.appendFile`. The proof harness loads the controller from source and drives three scenarios (pre-fix, post-fix, negative) against real temp session JSONL on disk.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```sh
  pnpm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  **(a) Three-scenario proof harness** — `run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs` loads the patched controller from source and drives all three scenarios against a real temp session JSONL on disk. Verbatim stdout:

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------
    [   0.12ms] setup      publishOwnedPostMessageWrite NOT called (pre-fix)
    [   0.15ms] lifecycle  releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    [   0.48ms] pi-write   appendFileSync direct to session JSONL (F0 → F1)
    [   0.61ms] hook       beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    [   1.72ms] hook       RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released)
    [   1.74ms] state      controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------
    [   0.02ms] setup      publishOwnedPostMessageWrite WIRED (post-fix)
    [   0.03ms] lifecycle  releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    [   0.15ms] pi-write   appendFileSync direct to session JSONL (F0 → F1)
    [   0.24ms] publish    publishOwnedPostMessageWrite() — record F1 as owned
    [   0.30ms] hook       beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    [   0.49ms] hook       RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)
    [   0.50ms] state      controller.hasSessionTakeover() = false

  -------- Scenario C — negative: external write bypasses publish (still trips) --------
    [   0.02ms] setup      publishOwnedPostMessageWrite NOT called (external write)
    [   0.03ms] lifecycle  releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    [   0.14ms] pi-write   appendFileSync direct to session JSONL (F0 → F1)
    [   0.23ms] hook       beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    [   0.43ms] hook       RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released)
    [   0.45ms] state      controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  ============================================================
    Scenario A (no fix):       hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario B (with fix):     hook fired=true, threw=no
    Scenario C (negative):     hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER

    RESULT: PASS
  ============================================================
  ```

  Scenario A confirms the bug exists pre-fix. Scenario B confirms the fix removes the false takeover for owned writes. **Scenario C is the bot-requested negative test** — external mutations that bypass `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` still trip the fence (fail-closed invariant preserved).

  **(b) Test suite** — 78 cases including 5 new regression tests:

  ```
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  78 passed (78)
  ```

  - `accepts pi-style writes published via publishOwnedPostMessageWrite before beforeToolCall fires` — positive case.
  - `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — **NEGATIVE case bot explicitly required**.
  - `allows multiple prompt turns with pi-style writes published per turn` — multi-turn, three iterations.
  - `does not classify a different session file's writes as owned by this controller` — cross-file isolation.
  - `does not hang when reacquireAfterPrompt rejects after a pi-style direct write` — abort/error path.

- **Observed result after fix:** All five new regression tests pass; `pnpm check:changed` clean.

- **What was not tested:** A live gateway-fallback reproduction against the patched build on real workload. The local repro that @cby2k has (session `4f181fe9-…`) is the most direct validation target; the patched build is being prepared on the user's GB10 node for that confirmation. The test suite covers the structural invariants at the unit level, and the proof harness exercises the actual controller machinery end-to-end against a real temp session file.

## Compatibility / Risk

- `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` is a new sync method on `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`. Only one call site (`onMessagePersisted` in `runEmbeddedAttempt`); not part of any external API.
- **Does not weaken takeover detection** — the trust gate (`isTrustedSessionFileState`) ensures only the lane's own pi-persistence writes get recorded as owned. External mutations that don't go through `onMessagePersisted` are never published and continue to trip the fence.
- `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite` is retained on the controller for existing call sites in tests; the production wiring no longer uses it from `onMessagePersisted`.
- `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock`'s signature is back to its original shape (no `refreshBeforeLock` param). No internal callers affected — single call site in `runEmbeddedAttempt`.

## Related

- Issue #86572 — root cause analysis and acceptance criteria
- #85913 — original `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` tracking issue; cby2k's deterministic repro
- #86067 — file-scoped prompt-window guard (cross-lane race) — independent fix; this PR addresses the same-lane race separately
- ClawSweeper review on `91ba92c9ab` flagged the need for the trust gate and the negative test; both included.

---

target.title: Fix stale approval prompts in Control UI
target.body:
Summary:
- Fix stale Control UI approval prompts when an exec/plugin approval is resolved from another client, such as Discord.
- Reuse manifest metadata during model visibility/catalog normalization so Control UI startup RPCs do not block the gateway during manual testing.
- Treat stale resolve responses as terminal UI cleanup signals: `APPROVAL_ALREADY_RESOLVED`, `APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND`, and `unknown or expired approval id` now dismiss the prompt and refresh pending approvals.
- Add a real Dismiss/close path that hides the modal without denying or approving the backend approval; approve/deny actions target the first non-dismissed visible prompt.
- Clear active prompt errors on resolved events, preserve in-flight local resolve state, and add expiry cleanup for approvals recovered from list refresh.

Bug summary:
A web approval modal could remain open after the same approval was resolved elsewhere. Acting on that stale modal could leave the prompt showing an error instead of dismissing it. The modal also needed a local close path so the user is not trapped in the approval dialog.

Root cause:
On main, `exec.approval.resolved` only removed the queue item and did not clear active modal error state. The approve/deny handler also treated all resolve errors as modal errors, so backend stale-approval responses stayed visible instead of clearing the stale prompt. The modal cancel path was wired to denial, so closing was not a neutral UI action.

Fix summary:
The approval controller now owns resolve and modal visibility cleanup. Success removes the active prompt. Stale backend errors remove it, refresh `exec.approval.list` and `plugin.approval.list`, and schedule expiry cleanup for refreshed items. A dismissed-id set lets users close the modal locally while keeping the backend approval pending; resolved/expired/refreshed state prunes that set.

Bug existed on main:
Yes. The worktree started from `origin/main` and the added regression failed before the fix. The rebased parent still had the same behavior: `removeResolvedApprovalRequest` only removed from `execApprovalQueue`, and `handleExecApprovalDecision` caught every resolve error as `execApprovalError`.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed, 87 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts` passed, 14 files / 115 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...changed files...` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-tsgo-core-approval.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-tsgo-test-src-approval.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n`: `pnpm check:changed` passed.

Behavior addressed: stale Control UI exec/plugin approval modals after external resolution, plus neutral modal dismissal without resolving the backend approval.
Real environment tested: local Vitest in the OpenClaw worktree plus Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 87 tests; Testbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n` exited 0.
Observed result after fix: stale resolve success/errors remove the modal, external resolved events clear active prompt errors, refreshed pending approvals expire from the UI, and Dismiss/Escape close the modal without sending a deny. Clean local OpenClaw manual-test Gateway stayed usable in Dia at `http://localhost:19171/`; clean-run logs showed `models.list` around 0.5-0.9s and `sessions.list`/`chat.history` around 0.8-1.0s on later reconnects.
What was not tested: a live Discord-to-Control-UI manual approval flow was not run.

Summary

- Add Signal native exec/plugin approval capability gated by top-level approvals configuration.
- Add thumbs-up / thumbs-down reaction approval handling for allow-once and deny, with explicit Signal approver auth.
- Bind reactions only from native approval delivery, verify target-author identity, persist binding state, and re-check current approval route config before resolving.
- Suppress duplicate local exec approval fallback messages after native Signal approval delivery succeeds.

Verification

- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts

- [x] Live prod Signal proof: real inbound `touch /tmp/foo` produced exactly one exec approval request and `/approve <id> allow-once` resolved it.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: synthetic exec approval prompt rendered `React with: 👍 Allow Once / 👎 Deny`; a real `signal-cli sendReaction --emoji 👍` against the exact bot-authored approval message resolved `allow-once`.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` temporarily empty, the approval prompt delivered without reaction choices and a 👍 reaction did not resolve within 6s.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` restored, a 👍 reaction sent to a non-matching target timestamp did not resolve within 6s.

Behavior addressed: Signal can deliver native approval prompts and resolve authorized 👍 / 👎 reactions as allow-once / deny without cross-conversation timestamp fallback or duplicate local exec approval fallback messages.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout, prod OpenClaw profile, real Signal channel via sender-side `signal-cli` receive/sendReaction artifacts with private account data redacted.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod claw-debug send-approval --type exec signal --to <redacted Signal target>`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> receive`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> sendReaction --emoji 👍 --target-author <redacted bot> --target-timestamp <approval-message-timestamp> <redacted bot>`; repeated with empty `channels.signal.allowFrom` and with a wrong target timestamp; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
Evidence after fix: authorized reaction proof returned `exec approval 634e7966-5721-40b8-abf6-72e03da4d318 approved with allow-once` and Signal received `✅ Exec approval allow-once. Resolved by Signal approval`; empty-allowFrom proof for `1c174127-f727-45af-a052-2664201c263e` stayed pending after 6s; wrong-target proof for `c1970b61-13ea-4a15-a93d-48e2b6b1387e` stayed pending after 6s; Showboat real-command proof passed with one post-send approval prompt and one final completion.
Observed result after fix: authorized exact-message Signal reactions resolve the intended approval, missing approvers do not get reaction choices and do not resolve by reaction, wrong-target reactions do not resolve the approval, and real Signal exec approval delivery no longer double-sends the local fallback prompt.
What was not tested: live plugin approval reaction roundtrip; plugin native rendering and reaction decision plumbing are covered by focused tests in this PR.

commands.author: ggzeng

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The scan logic (`scanSessionStoreForStaleRuntimeSnapshotPaths`) already computes both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath` for every finding. The repair replaces stale paths in the raw JSON text:

1. Add `shouldRepair?: boolean` to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` params
2. When `shouldRepair=true`: replace stale path prefixes with live bundled skills root
3. Handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes, unicode) via `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)`
4. For `baseDir` paths in `resolvedSkills`, also replace the directory prefix without `SKILL.md`
5. Create `.bak` backup before writing (mode 0o600)
6. Pass `ctx.prompter.shouldRepair` from `runSessionSnapshotsHealth()`

This follows the same pattern as other doctor repair functions (`noteSessionTranscriptHealth`, `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore`, `maybeScrubConfigAuditLog`).

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +60 lines (repair logic with JSON-escaped path handling)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script

## JSON-Escaped Path Handling

The repair logic in `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts:331-346` correctly handles JSON-escaped paths:

```typescript
// Lines 331-332: Generate JSON-escaped forms
const jsonEscaped = JSON.stringify(finding.cachedPath).slice(1, -1);
const jsonEscapedExpected = JSON.stringify(finding.expectedPath).slice(1, -1);

// Lines 336-340: Replace JSON-escaped form first (handles Windows backslashes)
if (fixed.includes(jsonEscaped)) {
  const occurrences = fixed.split(jsonEscaped).length - 1;
  fixed = fixed.replaceAll(jsonEscaped, jsonEscapedExpected);
  count += occurrences;
}

// Lines 342-346: Replace raw unescaped form as fallback
if (fixed.includes(finding.cachedPath)) {
  const occurrences = fixed.split(finding.cachedPath).length - 1;
  fixed = fixed.replaceAll(finding.cachedPath, finding.expectedPath);
  count += occurrences;
}
```

**Key implementation details:**
- `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` produces the JSON-escaped form (e.g., `C:\\Users\\user` becomes `C:\\\\Users\\\\user`)
- The repair tries JSON-escaped form first, then raw form as fallback
- This handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, and special characters correctly
- BaseDir paths in `resolvedSkills` also get directory prefix replacement (lines 350-365)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes) and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Live production instance with 14 sessions affected by pnpm runtime root change (see Issue #85689 for live output). Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**1. Live production instance (from Issue #85689):**

```
Session snapshots
- Found 14 sessions with stale cached session metadata paths.
  Live bundled skills root is healthy:
  ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  Cached session metadata still references an inactive runtime
  root; start a fresh session or reset the affected long-lived
  sessions after confirming history can be retired.
  Store: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  - agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_bc71ec62b088ed55e66fcc23c18a0056
    skillsSnapshot.prompt:
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20_@ty
  pes+express@5.0.6/.../clawhub/SKILL.md
    ->
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  nclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md
  ...and 209 more stale cached paths.
```

**2. Standalone reproduction script output (4 scenarios):**

```
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Real Behavior Proof: Doctor Session Snapshot Auto-Repair
Date: 2026-05-25T18:16:28.994Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Scenario 1: WITHOUT repair — findings computed but not applied
  Findings computed: 3 stale paths
  Repair performed: NO (shouldRepair not enabled)
  Stale paths remaining: 3
  PASS: findings detected, no repair

Scenario 2: WITH repair — paths replaced correctly
  Paths replaced: 3
  Backup created: true
  JSON valid: true
  Sessions preserved: 2
  Stale paths remaining: 0
  PASS: repair applied, JSON valid, backup created, no stale paths

Scenario 3: Windows backslash paths — JSON-escaped in file
  Input: Windows path with backslashes
  JSON-escaped form: C:\\Users\\user\\.local\\share\\pnpm\\gl...
  Repaired path contains live root: true
  Repaired path contains stale root: false
  PASS: Windows backslash paths repaired correctly

Scenario 4: Idempotent — second run finds nothing
  Second scan findings: 0
  PASS: no stale paths after repair

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Without repair: findings computed, stale paths persist
  With repair:    paths replaced, JSON valid, backup created
  Windows paths:  JSON-escaped backslash paths handled correctly
  Idempotent:     second scan finds nothing to repair

  Results: 4 passed, 0 failed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 4 scenarios pass. The live production instance (Issue #85689) shows the exact real-world scenario this PR addresses: 14 sessions with 223 stale paths after pnpm runtime root change. The repair logic correctly handles POSIX paths, Windows backslash paths (JSON-escaped), and baseDir directory paths. The repair is idempotent (Scenario 4). JSON validity is preserved after rewriting.

**What was not tested:**
- Live `openclaw doctor --fix` on the affected instance (requires building from PR branch — local build fails due to missing native bindings in container environment, documented in AGENTS.md)
- Concurrent access to sessions.json during repair (single-threaded doctor execution)

**Proof coverage:**
The standalone script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts:331-365`:
- JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1) for JSON-escaped path generation
- Try JSON-escaped form first, then raw form as fallback
- Handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, special characters
- Backup creation before write
- JSON validity preservation after repair

target.title: fix(gateway): filter transcript-only history rows
target.body:
Fixes #85669

Summary:
- Filters transcript-only OpenClaw assistant rows from `sessions_history` and session-history snapshot/SSE views.
- Keeps generic chat display projection unchanged so live OpenClaw injected assistant rows remain visible where they are part of delivery UI.
- Fetches a wider raw history window before filtering, then applies the user-visible limit after transcript/tool filtering.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_history` and session-history snapshot/SSE views no longer surface OpenClaw transcript-only assistant rows from `delivery-mirror` or `gateway-injected`, while generic chat display still keeps OpenClaw injected assistant rows.

Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout at commit `fb25677207a3017beb92ae1e8dcfaec25a2bead4` in `/Users/godcorn/cursor/open-source/projects/openclaw-85669`, using the repository's Node/Vitest runtime and the local gateway HTTP e2e coverage for session history.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts

```

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/godcorn/cursor/open-source/projects/openclaw-85669
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  18 passed (18)
Start at  01:21:55
```

target.title: fix(discord): fall back when voice adapter is unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord cron announce deliveries can fail with `discordVoice outbound adapter is unavailable` when automatic TTS marks a text payload as voice audio.
- Solution: keep the Discord voice path first, but fall back to normal Discord text when the unavailable `discordVoice` adapter error occurs and visible text is present.
- What changed: preserve reply targeting across the fallback by passing both `replyTo` and `replyToId`, and teach the Discord text sender to accept `replyToId` as an alias.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no cron/core routing change; real Discord voice sends still run first; non-unavailable voice errors and media-only voice payloads still fail instead of being masked.

## Motivation

- Fixes message loss for text-only Discord cron announcements when automatic TTS selects a voice delivery path but the isolated cron context has no Discord voice outbound adapter.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84952
- Related #84952
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord `audioAsVoice` payload delivery no longer stops at `discordVoice outbound adapter is unavailable` when visible text can be delivered through the normal Discord text path, and the fallback preserves the Discord reply target.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on PR head `c0a3c730369032e1b8cd2bd83ab4aa42f259773f`, sending through a real sandbox Discord text channel via the Discord API. The proof injects only the exact unavailable `discordVoice` error; the fallback `discord` text sender is not mocked and uses the real Discord outbound path. Channel id, bot token, and message ids are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-84952-worktree.XdsWKl
export OPENCLAW_DISCORD_SMOKE_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted sandbox bot token>
export OPENCLAW_DISCORD_SMOKE_CHANNEL_ID=<redacted sandbox text channel id>
./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/pr84965/real-discord-fallback-proof.mts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from the real Discord outbound run and API readback, copied live output with ids redacted:

```json
{
  "proof": "openclaw-pr-84965-real-discord-outbound-fallback",
  "channel": "<redacted>",
  "nonce": "1779375505200-9b7d02",
  "voiceUnavailablePathAttempted": 1,
  "seedMessage": {
    "created": true,
    "id": "<redacted:seed>",
    "content": "OpenClaw PR #84965 proof seed 1779375505200-9b7d02"
  },
  "fallbackMessage": {
    "created": true,
    "id": "<redacted:fallback>",
    "content": "OpenClaw PR #84965 proof fallback visible text 1779375505200-9b7d02",
    "replyTargetMatchesSeed": true,
    "attachmentCount": 0
  },
  "result": {
    "channel": "discord",
    "messageId": "<redacted:fallback>",
    "channelId": "<redacted>"
  }
}
```

Independent Discord API readback of the latest channel messages filtered by the same nonce:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "nonce": "1779375505200-9b7d02",
  "matchingProofMessages": 2,
  "seedPresent": true,
  "fallbackPresent": true,
  "fallbackReplyTargetMatchesSeed": true,
  "fallbackAttachmentCount": 0,
  "ids": {
    "seed": "<redacted>",
    "fallback": "<redacted>"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the unavailable voice-adapter path was attempted once, the fallback sent visible Discord text through the real Discord outbound path, Discord readback showed the fallback message replied to the intended seed message, and the fallback had `attachmentCount: 0` so the generated TTS media was not attached as normal media.
- What was not tested: a scheduled cron job was not run end-to-end in this sandbox; this proof exercises the Discord outbound `sendPayload` path with the exact unavailable `discordVoice` error and real Discord text delivery/readback. The existing regression test covers the cron-style `audioAsVoice` payload shape and reply-target propagation at the adapter seam.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
discordVoice outbound adapter is unavailable.
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord payload delivery treats automatic TTS media as `audioAsVoice` and resolves `discordVoice`; isolated cron/default outbound deps can expose a lazy `discordVoice` sender even when no Discord voice adapter is registered, so delivery throws before normal Discord text delivery runs.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing Discord `audioAsVoice` coverage only covered the available voice helper path, not the unavailable lazy-adapter path with visible text fallback.
- Contributing context (if known): the fallback must preserve both Discord-native reply options and generic runtime reply options because the default channel sender reads `replyToId` / `replyToMessageId`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: an `audioAsVoice` payload with visible text and an unavailable `discordVoice` dep falls back to the Discord text sender, sends no generated voice media as regular media, and preserves `replyTo` plus `replyToId`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the Discord outbound plugin boundary and dependency contract, so this catches the failing path without changing cron/core routing.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord text announcements that were previously lost when the voice adapter was unavailable now deliver as normal Discord text when visible payload text exists.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord cron announce + auto TTS -> audioAsVoice -> unavailable discordVoice -> delivery fails

After:
Discord cron announce + auto TTS -> audioAsVoice -> unavailable discordVoice -> Discord text fallback -> delivered text
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node.js checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord outbound plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): local test config only; no live Discord token used

### Steps

1. Reproduce the pre-fix failure shape with a Discord `audioAsVoice` payload and unavailable `discordVoice` sender.
2. Run the after-fix plugin-boundary proof script.
3. Run targeted Discord extension tests and the extensions type lane.

### Expected

- Visible text falls back to normal Discord text delivery when only the voice adapter is unavailable.
- The generated TTS media is not attached as regular media.
- Reply targeting is preserved.

### Actual

- The fallback text sender received the expected text, `replyTo`, `replyToId`, and no `mediaUrl`.
- Targeted Discord tests and type checks passed.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts` → 36 tests passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/send.sends-basic-channel-messages.test.ts` → 43 tests passed.
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` → passed.
  - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` → clean.
  - `./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/openclaw-84952-after-fix-proof.mts` → produced the fallback JSON shown above.
- Edge cases checked: fallback only handles the known unavailable `discordVoice` adapter error; the code still rethrows non-unavailable voice errors and does not fall back for media-only voice payloads without visible text.
- What you did **not** verify: a real scheduled cron execution. The dedicated Real behavior proof above covers the outbound fallback through a real sandbox Discord text-channel send plus Discord API readback; GitHub checks ran on the PR branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a real Discord voice send error could be hidden by fallback.
  - Mitigation: the fallback catches only the known unavailable `discordVoice` adapter message; all other voice send errors are rethrown.
- Risk: fallback text could lose reply targeting in the generic sender path.
  - Mitigation: the fallback passes both `replyTo` and `replyToId`, and the Discord text sender accepts `replyToId` as an alias.

target.title: fix(agents): skip wildcard catalog metadata refs
target.body:
## Summary

Skips wildcard default model allowlist entries while building concrete model catalog metadata and configured `models list` rows.

Entries such as `openai-codex/*` are valid allowlist selectors, but they are not concrete model ids and do not have per-model alias metadata to resolve or display as configured rows. The catalog metadata builder and configured list resolver now ignore those wildcard keys instead of sending them through concrete model normalization.

Split out from #85817 so the policy-scoping PR remains policy-only.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.configured.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 3 files, 9 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.configured.ts src/commands/models/list.configured.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/models/list.configured.ts src/commands/models/list.configured.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: wildcard default model allowlist entries are treated as selectors, not concrete catalog model ids or configured model rows.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-86524`, with a temp OpenClaw home at `/tmp/openclaw-86524-proof`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-86524-proof OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts models list --json`

Evidence after fix: the temp config set `agents.defaults.model` to `openai/gpt-5.5` and included both `agents.defaults.models["openai-codex/*"] = {}` and `agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.5"].alias = "Primary"`.

Observed result after fix: the redacted JSON summary was `{ "modelCount": 1, "keys": ["openai/gpt-5.5"], "wildcardRows": [], "configuredRows": [{ "key": "openai/gpt-5.5", "tags": ["default", "configured", "alias:Primary"], "aliases": [] }] }`.

What was not tested: live provider catalog discovery with external provider credentials.

target.body:
Summary

- Add Signal native exec/plugin approval capability gated by top-level approvals configuration.
- Add thumbs-up / thumbs-down reaction approval handling for allow-once and deny, with explicit Signal approver auth.
- Bind reactions only from native approval delivery, verify target-author identity, persist binding state, and re-check current approval route config before resolving.
- Suppress duplicate local exec approval fallback messages after native Signal approval delivery succeeds.

Verification

- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts
- [x] pnpm build
- [x] git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
- [x] uvx showboat verify .mem/main/proofs/demo-29-prod-signal-single-exec-approval/raw/showboat-summary.md
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: real inbound `touch /tmp/foo` produced exactly one exec approval request and `/approve <id> allow-once` resolved it.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: synthetic exec approval prompt rendered `React with: 👍 Allow Once / 👎 Deny`; a real `signal-cli sendReaction --emoji 👍` against the exact bot-authored approval message resolved `allow-once`.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` temporarily empty, the approval prompt delivered without reaction choices and a 👍 reaction did not resolve within 6s.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` restored, a 👍 reaction sent to a non-matching target timestamp did not resolve within 6s.

Behavior addressed: Signal can deliver native approval prompts and resolve authorized 👍 / 👎 reactions as allow-once / deny without cross-conversation timestamp fallback or duplicate local exec approval fallback messages.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout, prod OpenClaw profile, real Signal channel via sender-side `signal-cli` receive/sendReaction artifacts with private account data redacted.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod claw-debug send-approval --type exec signal --to <redacted Signal target>`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> receive`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> sendReaction --emoji 👍 --target-author <redacted bot> --target-timestamp <approval-message-timestamp> <redacted bot>`; repeated with empty `channels.signal.allowFrom` and with a wrong target timestamp; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
Evidence after fix: authorized reaction proof returned `exec approval 634e7966-5721-40b8-abf6-72e03da4d318 approved with allow-once` and Signal received `✅ Exec approval allow-once. Resolved by Signal approval`; empty-allowFrom proof for `1c174127-f727-45af-a052-2664201c263e` stayed pending after 6s; wrong-target proof for `c1970b61-13ea-4a15-a93d-48e2b6b1387e` stayed pending after 6s; Showboat real-command proof passed with one post-send approval prompt and one final completion.
Observed result after fix: authorized exact-message Signal reactions resolve the intended approval, missing approvers do not get reaction choices and do not resolve by reaction, wrong-target reactions do not resolve the approval, and real Signal exec approval delivery no longer double-sends the local fallback prompt.
What was not tested: live plugin approval reaction roundtrip; plugin native rendering and reaction decision plumbing are covered by focused tests in this PR.

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Keep CLI lazy outbound dependency routing from treating Discord voice helper keys as normal channel adapters.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping, plus a changelog fix entry for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 106 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node 22 with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not run for this source-level fix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The new CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 106 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff check, and the branch was rebased onto current `main` with an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run before opening this PR; the proof is focused source-level regression coverage for the broken dependency lookup path.

Fixes #84952

target.title: fix(openai): allow RFC 2544 fake-IP range for Realtime session requests
target.body: ## Summary - \`fetchWithSsrFGuard\` in \`createOpenAIRealtimeSecret\` was missing \`policy: { allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange: true }\`, causing the SSRF guard to block requests when proxy stacks (Clash, Surge, sing-box in TUN/fake-IP mode) resolve \`api.openai.com\` to the \`198.18.0.0/15\` special-use range. - The same opt-in already exists on other provider HTTP call paths; this fix closes the gap for the Realtime session-secret fetch path. - Added \`policy\` field assertions to both existing fetch-request checks in \`realtime-voice-provider.test.ts\` to prevent silent regression. Closes part of #56535. ## Verification \`\`\` pnpm test extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts \`\`\` - Test Files: 1 passed - Tests: 48 passed ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: SSRF guard blocks OpenAI Realtime session-secret requests when a TUN/fake-IP proxy resolves \`api.openai.com\` → \`198.18.0.0/15\` Real environment tested: macOS 15, OpenClaw 2026.5.25 (e3b9c11), Clash TUN/fake-IP mode active Exact steps or command run after this patch: \`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-dev pnpm gateway:dev --port 19001\`; open UI at \`http://127.0.0.1:19001\`; click Realtime Voice button Evidence after fix: \`talk.client.create\` RPC returned \`ok: true\` with \`durationMs: 1711\` (real network round-trip); audio streaming active with continuous \`talk.session.appendAudio\` calls all \`ok: true\`; AI responded in realtime — browser DevTools console: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87a884d2-2902-4915-ade1-99993691d5da Observed result after fix: Realtime voice session fully established; no SSRF block error; AI conversation active (see screenshots below) What was not tested: live Surge / sing-box environments (same code path, same policy opt-in) **Browser DevTools — session creation (`talk.client.create` → `ok: true`):** 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
